hotels.name: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
hotels.notes: 5 min walk from Shinjuku Station South Exit

hotels.name: Fujiya Hotel (est. 1878)
hotels.onsen_description: Historic mineral pools - indoor and outdoor rotenburo
hotels.notes: Japan's oldest Western hotel. Yukata and geta provided. 3 min walk from Miyanoshita Station.

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
hotels.notes: 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street. Free late-night ramen served 9:30-11 PM nightly.

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX
hotels.onsen_description: Rooftop natural hot spring with city views
hotels.notes: Lawson convenience store attached. Free late-night ramen 9:30-11 PM. Rooftop onsen open til midnight.

hotels.name: Residence Hotel Takayama Station
hotels.notes: 30 seconds from Takayama Station. Full apartment with kitchen.

hotels.name: Yuen Shinjuku
hotels.onsen_description: Rooftop onsen with indoor and outdoor baths - stunning Tokyo skyline views
hotels.notes: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station. Yukata provided. Rooftop onsen with Shinjuku skyline views.

onsens.type: Historic mineral pools - indoor and outdoor rotenburo
onsens.notes: First onsen of the trip! Historic mineral baths used since 1878.

onsens.type: Mountain mineral bath at sunrise - rotenburo
onsens.notes: Dawn rotenburo - almost empty at this hour.

onsens.type: Riverside rotenburo in cedar forest - public onsen
onsens.notes: Public forest onsen - taxi from Fujiya Hotel.

onsens.type: Last mountain mineral bath before departing for Kyoto
Natural hot spring with city views - open til midnight
onsens.notes: Follow up with free late-night ramen downstairs!

onsens.type: Indoor and outdoor baths with Tokyo skyline views
onsens.notes: Best onsen of the trip for views.

trains.seat_tip: Any seat - sit back and enjoy first views of Japan
trains.notes: Activate JR Pass at Narita JR counter before boarding.

trains.seat_tip: Seat 1A or 1B for panoramic front view. RIGHT side for Mt Fuji at 40 min mark.
trains.book_at: odakyu.jp - book at least 2 weeks ahead
trains.notes: Mt Fuji visible on right side around 40 min in.

trains.seat_tip: RIGHT side D seats - Mt Fuji visible around 10:20 AM. THIS IS THE LAST MT FUJI VIEW UNTIL RETURN.
trains.book_at: JR office at Odawara Station on the morning
trains.notes: Fully reclining seats, meal + drinks, hot towel, amenity kit. Only 18 Gran Class seats per train!
Transfer at Nagoya for Wide View Hida.

trains.seat_tip: LEFT side A/B seats - Hida River gorge appears on left side about 90 min in.
trains.notes: One of Japan's most scenic train journeys.

trains.seat_tip: LEFT side A/B seats - gorge views on return journey too
trains.notes: Transfer at Nagoya for Kintetsu to Suzuka, then evening Shinkansen to Tokyo.

trains.seat_tip: Any reserved seat - buy BOTH outbound and return at same time at Kintetsu counter.
trains.book_at: Kintetsu ticket office - EAST side of Nagoya Station. Buy both outbound + return together.
trains.notes: Approx ¥1,720 each way. Then 20-min Mie Kotsu shuttle bus from Shiroko to circuit. Suica accepted on Kintetsu.
trains.warning: NOT covered by JR Pass — buy separate Kintetsu ticket at Nagoya Station EAST exit. ~¥1,720 each way. Buy BOTH outbound + return at the same time.

trains.seat_tip: Any reserved seat - evening train after Suzuka
trains.book_at: JR office or machine at Nagoya Station on the day
trains.notes: Depart Nagoya around 7:10 PM, arrive Tokyo around 8:50 PM.
Depart 2:20 PM. Return Suica card at Narita JR office for deposit refund.

bookings_checklist.sale_date_japan: Sunday April 12, 2026 at 11:00 AM Japan Time (JST)
bookings_checklist.sale_date_chicago: Saturday April 11, 2026 at 9:00 PM Chicago Time (CDT)
bookings_checklist.notes: Buy Race Day Special Passport for May 23 + Pit Walk Pass. JR Pass does NOT cover Kintetsu to Shiroko — buy separately at Nagoya Station east exit.

bookings_checklist.where_to_book: Purchase before leaving Austin — cannot buy in Japan
JR office at Odawara Station on arrival morning

experiences_checklist.description: Traditional kimono rental and walk through Asakusa

experiences_checklist.item: Sumo Natsu Basho — Makuuchi top division
experiences_checklist.description: Watch Japan's top wrestlers at Ryogoku Kokugikan

experiences_checklist.item: Super Formula live racing at Suzuka Circuit + pit walk
experiences_checklist.description: Race Day Special Passport — walk the pitlane, watch the race live
JDM car culture mecca — moved to May 24 morning
Timed-entry immersive art — walk through water rooms

experiences_checklist.item: Chureito Pagoda — iconic Mt Fuji view
experiences_checklist.description: The postcard photo of Japan

experiences_checklist.item: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway + ring the love bell
experiences_checklist.description: Cable car over the volcano — ring the love bell at the top
Panoramic scenic train from Shinjuku to Hakone

experiences_checklist.item: Gran Class Shinkansen — Odawara to Kyoto
experiences_checklist.description: Japan's best train class — reclining seats, full meal, Mt Fuji view

experiences_checklist.item: Suzuka Circuit — walk the pitlane and watch Super Formula live
experiences_checklist.description: Walk where F1 cars race, watch Japan's top formula series
Historic mineral baths used since 1878
Riverside rotenburo in cedar forest

experiences_checklist.item: Authentic tea ceremony 90 min at Camellia
experiences_checklist.description: Traditional matcha tea ceremony in English

experiences_checklist.item: Samurai Museum — armor fitting, sword, shuriken
experiences_checklist.description: Try on samurai armor and throw shuriken
Walk through 10,000 red torii gates up the mountain
Japan's most photographed temple, covered in gold leaf
Walk Japan's most atmospheric geisha district at dusk
Historic castle with panoramic city views

experiences_checklist.item: Dotonbori neon canal + Glico Man couple photo
experiences_checklist.description: Osaka's famous neon strip along the canal
Historic Edo-period merchant streets and sake breweries
Open-air museum of traditional steep-roof farmhouses

experiences_checklist.item: Harajuku Cat Street — Supreme Japan, BAPE, Off-White
experiences_checklist.description: Tokyo's premier streetwear corridor

experiences_checklist.item: Takashimaya Times Square food hall + craft floor
experiences_checklist.description: Best department store food hall in Shinjuku
Pick up Yamazaki, Nikka, or Hibiki before flying home

documents.item: JR Pass vouchers x2 (purchased before leaving Austin)
Hotel confirmation printouts or saved offline
Suzuka Circuit Race Day Special Passport + Pit Walk Pass (May 23)

essentials.item: Portable WiFi / pocket WiFi (pre-book for Japan)
Universal power adapter (Japan uses Type A - same as US)
Cash in USD to exchange on arrival
Comfortable walking shoes (10,000+ steps per day)
Slip-on shoes for May 13 (kimono + teamLab water rooms)
Small daypack for TA-Q-BIN forwarding days (May 15 + May 21)
Small towel for onsen (provided at Fujiya and Dormy Inn)

clothing.item: Smart casual for Gran Class train and Fujiya Hotel dining
Easy-remove shoes for temples, tea ceremony, tatami rooms

tech.item: Camera + extra batteries and memory cards
Download Japan maps offline in Google Maps before leaving
Download Japanese offline translation pack in Google Translate

transport_tips: JR Pass covers all Shinkansen except Nozomi and Mizuho - always take Hikari or Kodama
Suica card works on all non-JR metro lines, buses, and even convenience stores
IC card readers everywhere - just tap in and tap out
Taxis are expensive but reliable - have hotel address in Japanese ready to show driver
Last train typically around midnight in major cities - do not miss it!
etiquette_tips: No eating or drinking while walking (except at food stalls and festivals)
Trash cans are rare - carry a small bag for waste
Quiet on trains - no phone calls, keep music in headphones
Onsen rules: shower thoroughly before entering, no swimwear, tattoos may be restricted at some
Cash is still widely used - always carry some yen
Bow when thanking someone - it is appreciated
Remove shoes when entering temples, traditional restaurants, and some museums

apps_to_download.name: Google Maps
apps_to_download.purpose: Navigation + download Japan offline maps before leaving

apps_to_download.name: Google Translate
apps_to_download.purpose: Camera translate menus and signs (download Japanese offline)

itinerary.title: Arrival — Welcome to Japan!

activities.name: Land at Narita Airport (NRT)
activities.description: Clear immigration, collect bags, exchange yen at airport bank counter.

activities.name: Activate JR Passes at Narita JR counter
activities.description: Find the JR East Travel Service Center before heading to trains. Show vouchers and passports.
activities.map_query: JR East Travel Service Center Narita Airport

activities.name: 🚃 N'EX Narita Express → Shinjuku (90 min)
activities.description: First real views of Japan out the window. JR Pass covered.
activities.transport: N'EX from Narita Airport Station — JR Pass

activities.name: Check in — JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
activities.description: 5 min walk from Shinjuku Station South Exit. Drop bags and get into trip mode.
activities.map_query: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku Tokyo

activities.name: First convenience store run
activities.description: 7-Eleven or Lawson — welcome to the best convenience stores on Earth. Onigiri, cold tea, Pocky.

activities.name: Shinjuku neon night walk
activities.description: Wander Kabukicho, the Golden Gai, and the east side lights. Try ramen or sushi for first dinner.

tips: Try to stay awake until 10 PM Japan time to beat jet lag.
Get a Suica IC card at any Narita JR machine — loads with cash, works on metros, buses, and at convenience stores.
The TMG (Tokyo Metropolitan Government) building observation deck is free and has stunning night views of Shinjuku.

itinerary.title: Asakusa, Kimono & teamLab Planets

activities.name: Asakusa — Senso-ji Temple + Nakamise Street
activities.description: Tokyo's oldest temple. Walk through the iconic Kaminarimon Thunder Gate and browse the shopping street.
activities.transport: Metro Ginza Line to Asakusa Station

activities.name: 🎌 BUCKET LIST — Kimono dress-up at Wargo Asakusa
activities.description: Full kimono rental with hair styling. Walk around Asakusa in traditional dress for 2+ hours.

activities.name: Lunch near Asakusa
activities.description: Tempura, soba, or sushi along Nakamise. Return kimono by 1:30 PM.

activities.name: 🎨 BUCKET LIST — teamLab Planets timed entry
activities.description: Immersive digital art in Toyosu — remove shoes, wade through water rooms. Completely surreal.
activities.transport: Yurikamome Line to Shin-Toyosu Station

activities.name: Shibuya Scramble Crossing
activities.description: Walk through the world's busiest pedestrian intersection. Get up high at Mag's Park for the overhead view.
activities.transport: Metro or JR to Shibuya Station

activities.name: Dinner in Shibuya or Shinjuku
activities.description: Izakaya night — try yakitori, kushikatsu, or ramen. Shinjuku east side has dozens of great spots.

tips: Wear slip-on shoes for kimono day — sandals are provided but easy-on/off helps.
Book teamLab well in advance — it sells out. Timed entry, so don't be late.
Wargo kimono rental: allow 45 min to get dressed and styled.

itinerary.title: Sumo Natsu Basho — Makuuchi Division

activities.name: Harajuku — Takeshita Street
activities.description: Quirky Japanese youth fashion street. Crepes, kawaii shops, vintage finds.
activities.transport: JR Yamanote Line to Harajuku Station

activities.name: Meiji Shrine
activities.description: Forested Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji. Peaceful contrast to Harajuku's chaos.

activities.name: Lunch in Omotesando
activities.description: Tokyo's Champs-Élysées — upscale shops and excellent restaurants. Try a basement food hall.

activities.name: Head to Ryogoku Kokugikan early
activities.description: Arrive before the top bouts to soak up atmosphere, grab a bento and beer inside.
activities.transport: JR Sobu Line to Ryogoku Station

activities.name: 🥊 BUCKET LIST — Sumo Natsu Basho, Makuuchi top division
activities.description: Top-ranked wrestlers compete in Japan's national sport. Ceremony, tradition, and raw power. The best Makuuchi bouts are the last ~10 of the day.

activities.name: Post-sumo chanko nabe dinner in Ryogoku
activities.description: The hot pot stew sumo wrestlers eat. Multiple chanko restaurants near the stadium.

tips: Makuuchi (top division) bouts start around 3 PM — be in your seat by then for atmosphere.
Top-ranked yokozuna and ozeki wrestle last — the final hour is the most exciting.
Buy a lacquered bento box inside the stadium — it's part of the experience.

itinerary.title: Romance Car to Hakone — Fujiya Hotel Check-In

activities.name: ⚠️ Send bags TA-Q-BIN → Kyoto (Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion)
activities.description: Ship bags via hotel concierge to your Kyoto hotel. They arrive next day. Pack only a daypack for 2 nights.
activities.map_query: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku Tokyo

activities.name: 🚃 BUCKET LIST — Odakyu Romance Car from Shinjuku
activities.description: Reserved panoramic train to Hakone. Sit RIGHT side seats 1A or 1B for Mt Fuji views ~40 min in. Book ahead!
activities.transport: Odakyu Romance Car from Shinjuku Odakyu Station — NOT JR Pass, ~¥1,500

activities.name: Arrive Hakone-Yumoto
activities.description: Switch to Hakone Tozan local train up towards Miyanoshita.

activities.name: Hakone Open-Air Museum
activities.description: Outdoor sculpture museum in the mountains. Includes a Picasso gallery.

activities.name: Lunch in Hakone
activities.description: Soba or udon near the museum or in Miyanoshita village.

activities.name: Check in — Fujiya Hotel (est. 1878)
activities.description: Japan's oldest Western hotel. Yukata and geta provided. Feel 145 years of history.

activities.name: Dinner at Fujiya Hotel dining room
activities.description: Historic dining room — smart casual. Western or Japanese set menu.

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN #1 — Fujiya Hotel historic mineral pools
activities.description: First onsen of the trip! Indoor and outdoor rotenburo using the same mineral springs since 1878.

tips: Sit RIGHT side seats 1A or 1B on Romance Car — Mt Fuji appears on the right ~40 min from Shinjuku.
TA-Q-BIN bags arrive at Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion the next day — pack very light for Hakone.
Fujiya's mineral water smells slightly sulphurous — that's normal and totally healthy!

itinerary.title: Chureito Pagoda, Mt Fuji Ropeway & Forest Onsen

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN #2 — Dawn rotenburo at Fujiya
activities.description: Almost empty at this hour. Mountain mineral bath at sunrise — magical light.

activities.name: Breakfast at Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: Western or Japanese set breakfast in the historic dining room.

activities.name: 🗻 BUCKET LIST — Chureito Pagoda
activities.description: Iconic five-story pagoda with Mt Fuji backdrop — the postcard shot of Japan. 398 steps from Arakurayama Sengen Park.
activities.transport: Fujikyu Bus or taxi towards Fujiyoshida

activities.name: Lunch near Kawaguchiko Lake
activities.description: Lake views and hoto noodles — Yamanashi's famous thick flat noodle soup.

activities.name: 🗻 BUCKET LIST — Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway + Ring the Love Bell
activities.description: Cable car over the volcano caldera with Mt Fuji behind you. Ring the love bell at Kachi Kachi Yama top station for good luck as a couple.
activities.map_query: Kachi Kachi Yama Ropeway Kawaguchiko Japan

activities.name: Return to Fujiya Hotel — rest
activities.description: Back to the hotel for an afternoon rest before the evening onsen trip.

activities.name: Dinner at Fujiya or Miyanoshita village
activities.description: Dinner at the hotel or explore Miyanoshita village restaurants.

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN #3 — Tenzan Tohji-kyo riverside forest onsen
activities.description: Public onsen in cedar forest beside a mountain river. Multiple indoor and outdoor baths. One of Hakone's best.
activities.transport: Taxi from Fujiya Hotel (~10 min, ~¥2,000 each way)

tips: Chureito gets crowded after 11 AM — go early and you'll have the view to yourself.
THIS IS YOUR LAST MT FUJI DAY — take it all in!
Tenzan onsen: bring a small towel. Tattoos may be restricted — check ahead.

itinerary.title: Gran Class Shinkansen to Kyoto

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN #4 — Fujiya final dawn soak
activities.description: Last soak in Fujiya's 1878 mineral springs. Say goodbye properly.

activities.name: Check out Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: Return yukata and geta. Bags were already shipped to Kyoto via TA-Q-BIN.

activities.name: Taxi or train to Odawara Station
activities.description: ~25 min taxi or Hakone Tozan train down to Odawara.

activities.name: Book Gran Class seats at JR Midori counter, Odawara
activities.description: Walk-up to JR ticket office. Use JR Pass + pay Gran Class supplement. Ask for RIGHT side D seats.

activities.name: 🚄 BUCKET LIST — Gran Class Shinkansen, Odawara → Kyoto (2 hrs)
activities.description: Only 18 seats per train. Full meal service, hot drinks, amenity kit, fully reclining. Sit RIGHT side D seats — Mt Fuji appears ~10:20 AM. THIS IS YOUR LAST MT FUJI VIEW OF THE TRIP.
activities.transport: Hikari Shinkansen — JR Pass + Gran Class supplement

activities.name: Arrive Kyoto Station — collect bags
activities.description: Head to Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion. TA-Q-BIN bags delivered from Shinjuku.
activities.transport: Taxi or city bus from Kyoto Station to Gion

activities.name: Check in — Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
activities.description: 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street. Free late-night ramen 9:30–11 PM every night.

activities.name: 🏮 BUCKET LIST — Gion Hanamikoji Street at dusk
activities.description: Walk Japan's most beautiful geisha district as the lanterns light up. Maiko walk to appointments ~5–6 PM.

activities.name: Dinner in Gion
activities.description: Kaiseki or a casual Kyoto izakaya in the Gion backstreets.

activities.name: Free late-night ramen at Dormy Inn
activities.description: Complimentary ramen served in the hotel lobby 9:30–11 PM every night. Don't skip it.

tips: Sit RIGHT side D seats on Gran Class — Mt Fuji appears left of Shizuoka around 10:20 AM.
This is your ABSOLUTE LAST MT FUJI VIEW of the trip. Camera out and ready.
Gion is best at dusk on weekdays — maiko walk to evening appointments between 5–6 PM.

itinerary.title: Tea Ceremony, Samurai Museum & Nijo Castle

activities.name: 🍵 BUCKET LIST — Camellia Tea Ceremony (90 min)
activities.description: Traditional matcha tea ceremony conducted in English. Learn whisking, bowing, sweets, and the way of tea.

activities.name: Nijo Castle
activities.description: UNESCO World Heritage shogun's castle. Walk the famous nightingale floors designed to squeak as a security alarm.

activities.name: Lunch in central Kyoto
activities.description: Ramen, soba, or obento near Nijo area.

activities.name: ⚔️ BUCKET LIST — Samurai Museum Kyoto
activities.description: Try on full samurai armor, hold a real katana, and throw shuriken. Hands-on English experience.

activities.name: Philosopher's Path afternoon walk
activities.description: Canal-side stone path lined with trees, connecting Nanzen-ji to Ginkaku-ji.

activities.name: Dinner in Gion
activities.description: Kyoto tofu kaiseki or atmospheric izakaya in the Gion backstreets.

activities.name: Free ramen at Dormy Inn
activities.description: Second night of complimentary late-night ramen.

tips: Book Camellia Tea Ceremony well in advance — English sessions fill up fast.
Nijo Castle's creaking nightingale floors were an early security alarm system.
Samurai Museum is genuinely fun — try everything, they encourage it!

itinerary.title: Fushimi Inari at Dawn & The Golden Pavilion

activities.name: ⛩️ BUCKET LIST — Fushimi Inari gates at dawn
activities.description: 10,000 vermillion torii gates climbing the mountain. Go at 6:30 AM — almost empty, gorgeous golden light. Gets very crowded after 9 AM.
activities.transport: JR Nara Line to Inari Station — JR Pass covered

activities.name: Return and breakfast
activities.description: Breakfast near Fushimi Inari or back at the hotel.

activities.name: 🥇 BUCKET LIST — Kinkaku-ji, The Golden Pavilion
activities.description: Japan's most photographed temple — covered in real gold leaf, perfectly reflected in a mirror pond.
activities.transport: City bus 101 or 205 from central Kyoto

activities.name: Arashiyama bamboo grove
activities.description: Walk through towering bamboo stalks — iconic Japanese scenery. Quieter at midday.
activities.transport: JR Sagano Line to Saga-Arashiyama Station — JR Pass

activities.name: Tenryu-ji Garden + Hozu River
activities.description: UNESCO heritage Zen garden with mountain backdrop. Rent a rowboat on the river.

activities.name: Ninenzaka + Sannenzaka cobbled lanes
activities.description: Last Kyoto evening — walk the stone-paved lanes near Kiyomizu-dera lined with craft shops.

activities.name: Final Kyoto dinner
activities.description: Make it special — try a sushi counter or proper kaiseki restaurant.

activities.name: Free ramen at Dormy Inn — last night in Kyoto
activities.description: Last complimentary ramen. Pack tonight for an early start tomorrow.

tips: Fushimi Inari at 6:30 AM is one of the best experiences in Japan — do NOT skip.
The full Inari hike takes ~2 hours round trip. You don't have to go all the way to the top.
Arashiyama: visit midday on a weekday to avoid the bamboo grove crowds.

itinerary.title: Kyoto to Osaka — Castle & Dotonbori

activities.name: Check out Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
activities.description: Check out and head to Kyoto Station.

activities.name: Hikari Shinkansen — Kyoto → Shin-Osaka (15 min)
activities.description: Shortest Shinkansen of the trip. JR Pass covered.
activities.transport: Hikari Shinkansen from Kyoto Station — JR Pass

activities.name: 🏯 BUCKET LIST — Osaka Castle, 8 floors
activities.description: Historic feudal castle with panoramic Osaka views from the observation deck. Museum inside.
activities.transport: Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line to Tanimachi 4-chome

activities.name: Lunch near Osaka Castle
activities.description: Takoyaki or ramen near the park. First taste of proper Osaka food culture.

activities.name: Check in — Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX
activities.description: Rooftop natural hot spring, Lawson convenience store attached, free ramen 9:30 PM.
activities.map_query: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX Osaka

activities.name: 🌉 BUCKET LIST — Dotonbori canal + Glico Man couple photo
activities.description: Osaka's famous neon strip. Stand in front of the Glico Man running sign for the classic couple photo.

activities.name: Kuromon Market
activities.description: Osaka's kitchen — 180+ stalls of fresh seafood, wagyu, street food. Eat everything.

activities.name: Dinner in Dotonbori — Osaka soul food
activities.description: Takoyaki, okonomiyaki, kushikatsu, fresh crab. Osaka is Japan's food capital.

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN #5 — Rooftop onsen Dormy Inn Namba (open til midnight)
activities.description: Natural hot spring with city views. Follow with free late-night ramen downstairs.
activities.map_query: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX Osaka rooftop

tips: Osaka food rule: eat everything. The local specialties here are takoyaki, okonomiyaki, and kushikatsu.
Dotonbori at night is overwhelming in the best possible way — neon, crowds, smells.
Rooftop onsen closes at midnight — plan for it!

itinerary.title: Osaka to Takayama — Scenic Mountain Train

activities.name: ⚠️ Send bags TA-Q-BIN → Tokyo (Yuen Shinjuku)
activities.description: Ship bags to Yuen Shinjuku via hotel concierge — arrive May 23 or 24. Pack daypack for 2 nights in Takayama.
activities.map_query: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX Osaka

activities.name: Shinsaibashi shopping arcade
activities.description: Japan's longest covered shopping arcade. Last Osaka walk — pick up omiyage (souvenirs).

activities.name: Hikari Shinkansen — Osaka → Nagoya (50 min)
activities.description: Quick hop to Nagoya for the connection. JR Pass covered.
activities.transport: Hikari Shinkansen from Shin-Osaka — JR Pass

activities.name: Transfer at Nagoya to Wide View Hida platform
activities.description: Walk across Nagoya Station to the JR limited express platforms. Grab bentos for the scenic journey.

activities.name: 🌄 Wide View Hida — Nagoya → Takayama (2h 25min)
activities.description: One of Japan's most scenic train journeys. Sit LEFT side A/B seats — Hida River gorge views appear ~90 min in.
activities.transport: Wide View Hida Limited Express — JR Pass covered

activities.name: Arrive Takayama — check in (30 sec from station!)
activities.description: Residence Hotel is 30 seconds from the station exit. Full apartment with kitchen.

activities.name: 🍶 BUCKET LIST — Sanmachi Suji old town + sake tastings
activities.description: Walk Takayama's perfectly preserved Edo-period merchant streets. Visit sake breweries for free tastings.

activities.name: Dinner in Takayama
activities.description: Try Hida beef (local Wagyu), mitarashi dango, or local soba.

tips: Sit LEFT side seats A/B on Wide View Hida — Hida River gorge is on the left side about 90 min in.
Takayama feels like stepping back 300 years — no flashing signs, no crowds.
Sake breweries: look for white cedar balls (sugidama) hanging outside — signals fresh sake inside.

itinerary.title: Takayama Full Day — Hida Folk Village

activities.name: Jinya-mae Morning Market
activities.description: Open-air market in front of Takayama Jinya. Fresh produce, pickles, crafts. Runs until noon.

activities.name: Takayama Jinya Historic Government House
activities.description: The only remaining feudal government outpost in Japan — rice granaries and interrogation rooms.

activities.name: 🏘️ BUCKET LIST — Hida Folk Village (Hida no Sato)
activities.description: Open-air museum of gassho-zukuri farmhouses transplanted from the mountains. Thatched roofs, folk crafts, stunning setting.
activities.transport: Bus from Takayama Station (~10 min, ~¥210)

activities.name: Lunch in Sanmachi Suji old town
activities.description: Mitarashi dango, Hida beef skewers, or local soba.

activities.name: Sanmachi Suji afternoon browse
activities.description: More sake tastings, antique shops, Hida lacquerware, souvenir hunting.

activities.name: Higashiyama Walking Course
activities.description: 2km peaceful temple and shrine walk through the forested eastern hills.

activities.name: Dinner — Hida beef tonight
activities.description: Splurge on Hida beef at a proper restaurant tonight — it is world-class local Wagyu.

activities.name: Pack for early tomorrow
activities.description: Check out is early — Suzuka day requires leaving at 7 AM. Lay out train tickets and Suzuka passes.

tips: Hida Folk Village is stunning on a clear day — mountains behind the farmhouses.
The morning market is genuine, not tourist-facing. Buy a pickled turnip.
Tonight is your last night in the Japanese Alps — big day tomorrow!

itinerary.title: 🏁 Suzuka Circuit — Super Formula Race Day → Tokyo

activities.name: Check out — Residence Hotel Takayama (30 sec to station)
activities.description: Early checkout. Walk 30 seconds to Takayama Station.

activities.name: Wide View Hida — Takayama → Nagoya (2h 25min)
activities.description: Sit LEFT side for Hida gorge views on the return. JR Pass covered.
activities.transport: Wide View Hida Limited Express — JR Pass

activities.name: Arrive Nagoya — head to Kintetsu EAST exit (NOT JR side)
activities.description: Do NOT go to the JR side. Walk through to the east side of Nagoya Station for the Kintetsu building.

activities.name: ⚠️ Buy Kintetsu tickets — NOT JR Pass, ~¥1,720 each way
activities.description: Buy BOTH outbound AND return tickets at the same time at the Kintetsu ticket window. ~¥3,440 total per person.
activities.map_query: Kintetsu Nagoya Station ticket office Japan

activities.name: Kintetsu Limited Express → Shiroko Station (50 min)
activities.description: Comfortable reserved seating to Shiroko — the nearest station to Suzuka Circuit.
activities.transport: Kintetsu Nagoya Line → Shiroko — NOT JR Pass

activities.name: Mie Kotsu shuttle bus → Suzuka Circuit (20 min)
activities.description: Bus runs directly from Shiroko Station to the circuit entrance during race weekends.
activities.transport: Mie Kotsu shuttle bus from Shiroko Station to Suzuka Circuit

activities.name: 🏎️ Arrive Suzuka Circuit — collect passes at gate
activities.description: Show Race Day Special Passport + Pit Walk Pass confirmation at the entrance.

activities.name: 🏎️ BUCKET LIST — PIT WALK at Suzuka Circuit
activities.description: Walk the actual pitlane of the legendary Suzuka F1 circuit. See cars up close, meet mechanics, stand where champions raced.

activities.name: Super Formula free practice / qualifying
activities.description: Japan's premier open-wheel series. Find grandstand seating and watch the fastest cars on circuit.

activities.name: 🏎️ BUCKET LIST — SUPER FORMULA RACE!
activities.description: Lights out! Live Super Formula race at Suzuka. The sound, the speed, the hairpin, the esses. Japan motorsport at its finest.

activities.name: Suzuka souvenir shop + depart circuit
activities.description: Grab a Suzuka Circuit or Super Formula souvenir before shuttling back to Shiroko.

activities.name: Shuttle bus back to Shiroko Station
activities.description: Board Mie Kotsu shuttle from circuit back to Shiroko.

activities.name: Kintetsu Limited Express → Nagoya (50 min)
activities.description: Use your pre-purchased return Kintetsu ticket.
activities.transport: Kintetsu back to Nagoya — NOT JR Pass

activities.name: Hikari Shinkansen — Nagoya → Tokyo (1h 40min)
activities.description: Evening train back to Tokyo. JR Pass covered.
activities.transport: Hikari Shinkansen from Nagoya — JR Pass

activities.name: Arrive Tokyo — check in Yuen Shinjuku
activities.description: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station. TA-Q-BIN bags should be waiting.

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN #6 — Yuen Shinjuku rooftop
activities.description: Indoor and outdoor baths with the full Tokyo skyline. Best onsen view of the trip.

tips: Kintetsu counter is on the EAST side of Nagoya Station — completely separate building from JR.
Buy BOTH outbound AND return Kintetsu tickets at the same time at the counter.
Race Day Special Passport includes pit walk access — confirm your pass at the gate on arrival.
The shuttle bus back fills up after the race — leave slightly early if you want breathing room.

itinerary.title: A-PIT Autobacs, Harajuku & Final Tokyo Day

activities.name: 🚗 BUCKET LIST — A-PIT Super Autobacs Shinonome (4 floors JDM)
activities.description: Japan's ultimate car culture destination. 4 floors of JDM parts, performance accessories, garage culture, and car exhibits. Heaven for car people.
activities.transport: Yurikamome Line to Shinonome Station (~20 min from Shinjuku)

activities.name: Lunch in Toyosu area
activities.description: Toyosu Market is nearby — incredible fresh sushi at the outer market.
activities.map_query: Toyosu Market outer market sushi Tokyo

activities.name: 🛍️ BUCKET LIST — Harajuku Cat Street streetwear run
activities.description: Walk Cat Street from Harajuku to Omotesando. Supreme Japan, BAPE, Off-White, Neighborhood, Undercover.
activities.transport: Metro or JR to Harajuku or Omotesando Station

activities.name: Omotesando flagship stores
activities.description: World-class flagship architecture — Louis Vuitton, Prada, Comme des Garçons, Issey Miyake.

activities.name: Takashimaya Times Square — craft + food hall scout
activities.description: Scout the craft floors and food hall basement for tomorrow's final run before flying.

activities.name: Shinjuku Golden Gai — tiny atmospheric bars
activities.description: 6 alleys, 200+ tiny bars each holding 5–8 people. The world's most unique bar crawl.

activities.name: Final stroll through Shinjuku
activities.description: Last full night in Japan. Absorb everything — lights, sounds, smells, energy.

activities.name: Pack for the flight home
activities.description: N'EX departs 2:20 PM tomorrow. Leave hotel by 12:30 PM. Pack tonight.

tips: A-PIT Autobacs opens at 10 AM — arrive right at opening before the Sunday crowds build.
Cat Street runs from Harajuku Station south towards Omotesando — walk the whole thing.
Golden Gai: just walk in and pick a bar with a vibe you like. Most welcome foreigners warmly.

itinerary.title: Sayonara — Fly Home to Austin 🇯🇵

activities.name: Check out — Yuen Shinjuku
activities.description: Return yukata. Check you have everything — JR Pass, passport, all receipts.

activities.name: 🛒 BUCKET LIST — Takashimaya Times Square food hall
activities.description: Last Japanese food haul. Wagashi sweets, freshly made onigiri, tamagoyaki rolls, artisan snacks for the flight.
activities.map_query: Takashimaya Times Square Shinjuku food hall Tokyo

activities.name: Final Shinjuku walk
activities.description: Last moments in Japan. One more convenience store run. Say goodbye.

activities.name: Head to Shinjuku Station — N'EX platform
activities.description: Grab bags from hotel, walk to Shinjuku Station for the 2:20 PM N'EX.
activities.map_query: Shinjuku Station N'EX Narita Express platform

activities.name: N'EX Narita Express — Shinjuku → Narita Airport (90 min)
activities.description: Last train ride of the trip. JR Pass covered.
activities.transport: N'EX from Shinjuku Station — JR Pass

activities.name: Arrive Narita Airport — return Suica cards
activities.description: Return Suica cards at the JR counter BEFORE going through security — ¥500 deposit refund each.

activities.name: 🥃 BUCKET LIST — Duty free Japanese whisky
activities.description: Yamazaki 12, Nikka from the Barrel, Hibiki Harmony — often cheaper at Narita than in the city.

activities.name: ✈️ Fly home to Austin — Sayonara Japan!
activities.description: 14 unforgettable days. You'll be back. 🇯🇵

tips: Return Suica cards at the JR counter BEFORE security — ¥500 deposit refund each card.
N'EX departs 2:20 PM sharp — leave the hotel by 12:30 PM at the latest.
Japanese whisky at Narita duty free is often the same price or cheaper than Shinjuku shops.
